title: Understand: gravitational force
description: Check your understanding of gravitational force in this set of free practice questions.

assessment_items.question_content:
Objects $\bold{A}$ and $\bold{B}$ exert gravitational forces on each other.

**Complete the statements.**

* The more mass $\bold{A}$ and $\bold{B}$ have, the [[☃ dropdown 1]] the gravitational forces they exert on each other.

* The farther apart $\bold{A}$ and $\bold{B}$ are, the [[☃ dropdown 2]] the gravitational forces they exert on each other.
hints: The strength of the gravitational forces two objects exert on each other depends on the objects' masses. The more mass one or both of the objects have, the stronger the gravitational forces they exert on each other.

The strength of the gravitational forces two objects exert on each other also depends on the distance between the objects. The farther apart the objects are, the weaker the gravitational forces they exert on each other.

The answer is:

* The more mass $\bold{A}$ and $\bold{B}$ have, the stronger the gravitational forces they exert on each other.

* The farther apart $\bold{A}$ and $\bold{B}$ are, the weaker the gravitational forces they exert on each other.

choices.content: weaker
stronger
weaker
stronger

assessment_items.question_content:
Objects $\bold{A}$ and $\bold{B}$ exert gravitational forces on each other.

**Complete the statements.**

* The less mass $\bold{A}$ and $\bold{B}$ have, the [[☃ dropdown 1]] the gravitational forces they exert on each other.

* The closer together $\bold{A}$ and $\bold{B}$ are, the [[☃ dropdown 2]] the gravitational forces they exert on each other.
hints: The strength of the gravitational forces two objects exert on each other depends on the objects' masses. The less mass one or both of the objects have, the weaker the gravitational forces they exert on each other.

The strength of the gravitational forces two objects exert on each other also depends on the distance between the objects. The closer together the objects are, the stronger the gravitational forces they exert on each other.

The answer is:

* The less mass $\bold{A}$ and $\bold{B}$ have, the weaker the gravitational forces they exert on each other.

* The closer together $\bold{A}$ and $\bold{B}$ are, the stronger the gravitational forces they exert on each other.

choices.content: weaker
stronger
weaker
stronger

assessment_items.question_content:
Objects $\bold{A}$ and $\bold{B}$ exert gravitational forces on each other.

**Complete the statement.**

* The gravitational forces are stronger when $\bold{A}$ and $\bold{B}$ have [[☃ dropdown 3]] and are [[☃ dropdown 4]].
hints: The strength of the gravitational forces two objects exert on each other depends on the objects' masses. The more mass one or both of the objects have, the stronger the gravitational forces they exert on each other.

The strength of the gravitational forces two objects exert on each other also depends on the distance between the objects. The farther apart the objects are, the weaker the gravitational forces they exert on each other.

The answer is:

* The gravitational forces are stronger when $\bold{A}$ and $\bold{B}$ have more mass and are closer together.

choices.content: less mass
more mass
closer together
farther apart

assessment_items.question_content:
Objects $\color{red}{\bold{A}}$ and $\color{blue}{\bold{B}}$ exert gravitational forces on each other. $\color{red}{\bold{A}}$ has more mass than $\color{blue}{\bold{B}}$.

**Which model correctly shows the relative gravitational force strengths?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: ![A larger circle labelled A and a smaller circle labelled B. An arrow points from A towards B, and another arrow of equal length points from B towards A.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/5eb9d2b7d2d8d7b12c34edf22ae83a98.svg)

![A larger circle labelled A and a smaller circle labelled B. A longer arrow points from A towards B, and another shorter arrow points from B towards A.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/23bd6c9967f56ad0ac7af64339b6c7e5.svg)

![A larger circle labelled A and a smaller circle labelled B. A shorter arrow points from A towards B, and another longer arrow points from B towards A.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/a797e7b6a9e186ac8b83ddd12805725b.svg)

assessment_items.question_content:
**Select the true statements about gravity.**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Two objects must be in contact to exert gravitational forces on each other.
Gravitational forces are always attractive.
Any two masses exert gravitational forces on each other.
There is no gravity in outer space.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Select the true statements about gravity.**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Two objects can exert gravitational forces on each other without being in contact.
Gravitational forces can either be attractive or repulsive.
Only very massive objects like stars and planets exert gravitational forces on each other.
Gravity is present in outer space.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Select the true statements about gravity.**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Two objects can exert gravitational forces on each other without being in contact.
Gravitational forces can either be attractive or repulsive.
Any two masses exert gravitational forces on each other.
There is no gravity in outer space.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Select the true statements about Earth's gravity.**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Earth's gravity is approximately the same at the top of a tall mountain compared to ground level.
Astronauts orbiting Earth do not experience a gravitational force from Earth.
The direction of Earth's gravitational force on an object near Earth depends on the direction the object is moving.
Earth's gravitational field is present in all the space around Earth, whether another object is located there or not.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Select the true statements about Earth's gravity.**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Earth's gravity is significantly weaker at the top of a mountain compared to ground level.
Astronauts orbiting Earth experience a gravitational force from Earth.
The direction of Earth's gravitational force on all objects near Earth is down, towards Earth's center.
Earth's gravitational field is only present in spots around Earth where other objects are located.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Select the true statements about Earth's gravity.**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Earth's gravity is significantly weaker at the top of a mountain compared to ground level.
Astronauts orbiting Earth do not experience a gravitational force from Earth.
The direction of Earth's gravitational force on all objects near Earth is down, towards Earth's center.
Earth's gravitational field is present in all the space around Earth, whether another object is located there or not.
